rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate boolean helper declarations for range checks matching the actual data types of visible features.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Use helper variables to encapsulate complex boolean expressions for clarity and reuse.
Ensure type consistency between AGREE expressions and AADL feature types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Component type contracts use feature names directly; implementation contracts use subcomponent ports or declared variables.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the expected data types of the features involved.
Assign statements are only valid for output features or auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope unless they are bindings to outputs.
Do not reference AADL property names as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not assume Boolean ports can be treated as integers without explicit scalar representation.
Do not generate range assumptions without concrete bounds or visible feature names.
